_note: Round-12 follow-up #2: destructure defaults `{ a = 'fallback' }` should narrow the projected type to remove undefined. Pre-fix native projected `label` as `Row['label']` = `string | undefined` (because `label?: string` is optional in Row), so consumer-side `takesString(label)` would have failed TS2345 (`string | undefined` not assignable to `string`). Upstream's IIFE `(({ label = 'x' }) => label)(source)` narrows: when source.label is undefined, the default kicks in; either way the local has type `string`. Native now tracks `has_default: bool` per BoundIdent (set by AssignmentPattern walk) and wraps the projected leaf in `Exclude<…, undefined>` when set. Producer here exposes `label: Exclude<Row['label'], undefined>` = `string`; Consumer's `takesString(label)` now passes. (The default's literal type 'fallback' isn't tracked — if the source type were e.g. `'a' | 'b' | undefined`, native would give `'a' | 'b'` while upstream gives `'a' | 'b' | 'fallback'`. Acknowledged divergence.)
